commands.review_summary:
The PR updates WebChat history loading to arm `reconnectResumeSessionId` after `chat.history` returns an existing session, updates the UI send expectation, and adds session reset regression coverage for ordinary stale sends.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +67. Total +70 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main loads the history-backed session id but does not arm the resume marker, while the backend only suppresses stale rollover when that marker is present. I did not rerun a live WebChat restart locally in this read-only review, but I inspected the contributor's screenshots and terminal proof.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair remains; the next action is maintainer review of the loaded-history session-state policy tradeoff before merge.

target.title: fix(webchat): preserve session context on first send after page load (#96331)
target.body: ## Summary WebChat `chat.send` now preserves session context on the first send after page load by setting `reconnectResumeSessionId` when `chat.history` loads an existing session from disk, closing the gap where `chat.history` displayed full history but `chat.send` lost model-visible context after gateway restart. - **Problem**: `reconnectResumeSessionId` was only set on WebSocket close (`onClose`), so a fresh page load after gateway restart (or after crossing the daily reset boundary) would display full history via `chat.history` (which reads the transcript directly from disk) but `chat.send` would independently evaluate session freshness via `initSessionState`, mark the session stale, and mint a new empty session. The backend `resumeRequestedSession` infrastructure (from PR #89017) already handles the gateway side — only the UI signal was missing. - **Solution**: After `chat.history` loads a session that exists on disk, set `reconnectResumeSessionId = currentSessionId`. The first `chat.send` then carries the one-shot `__controlUiReconnectResume` signal, which flows through the existing `resumeRequestedSession` → `reconnectResumeRequested` path in `initSessionState`. Subsequent sends clear the flag and follow normal freshness evaluation. - **What changed**: `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts` (+9/-5: set `reconnectResumeSessionId` after session load instead of only clearing on mismatch), `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts` (+5/-3: updated test expectation), `src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts` (+66: 2 regression tests proving ordinary stale sends still roll over) - **What did NOT change**: Backend session init (`session.ts`), gateway dispatch (`chat.ts`), `MsgContext` type, plugin SDK exports, config, migration paths, `/new` and `/reset` behavior, heartbeat/system events ## Change Type & Scope ### Change Type - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ### Scope - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue - Closes #96331 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Motivation Web UI users returning to a chat session after gateway restart (most commonly noticed the next morning after the 4 AM daily reset boundary) would see their full chat history displayed correctly via `chat.history`, but the model would respond as if it had no prior context. The root cause was a UI-side gap: `reconnectResumeSessionId` was only set on WebSocket close, never on fresh page load. ## Review Contract ```text Ref: #96331 / PR #96391 Surface: Web UI / session init / chat.send dispatch Bug: chat.history shows session history but chat.send loses it after gateway restart Cause: reconnectResumeSessionId was only set on WebSocket close (onClose), so a fresh page load after gateway restart never carried the resume signal. The backend resumeRequestedSession infrastructure (PR #89017, already in main) was complete but unreachable from the fresh-page-load path. (ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts:758-763) Confidence: confirmed by code analysis + reproducible scenario Provenance: introduced by: gap in PR #89017 (UI-side: only set resume marker on socket close) made visible by: gateway restart + daily reset boundary crossing Confidence: clear Best fix: Yes. Setting reconnectResumeSessionId after every chat.history load is the minimal correct fix. The resume signal is one-shot (cleared after first send), so subsequent sends follow normal freshness evaluation. No changes to MsgContext, plugin SDK types, or backend session logic. Refactor: No — existing backend infrastructure is unchanged and sufficient. Proof: 31 UI tests + 126 session tests pass (4 new), build succeeds (pnpm build). See Real Behavior Proof below for terminal output. Risk: Low. The one-shot flag is cleared in requestChatSend (existing behavior). /new and /reset are unaffected (isNewSession guard takes precedence in session.ts). Heartbeat/system events use independent isSystemEvent guard. ``` ## Real Behavior Proof **Behavior addressed**: Web UI `chat.history` displays full history but `chat.send` reaches model without context after gateway restart (cross-day or same-day). After fix: first `chat.send` after page load carries `__controlUiReconnectResume`, gateway reuses existing session, model receives full context. **Real environment tested**: Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, Node 22.19+, branch `fix/webchat-session-context-restart-96331-v2`, rebased on upstream/main (7c47904bb4) **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: ```bash # UI tests — resume/reconnect/history pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts --run -t "reconnect|resume|history" ``` ``` Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 31 passed | 97 skipped (128) ``` ```bash # Session tests — new regression tests pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts --run -t "rolls over.*ordinary|reuses.*idle-expired.*reconnect|does not reuse.*stale asserted" ``` ``` Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 4 passed | 122 skipped (126) ``` ```bash # Full session regression suite pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts --run ``` ``` Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 126 passed (126) ``` ```bash # Gateway chat.send tests pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.send-deleted-agent.test.ts --run ``` ``` Test Files 2 passed (2) Tests 2 passed (2) ``` ```bash # Build pnpm build ``` (exit code 0, clean) **Evidence after fix**: Total: 159 tests passed across 4 test files, 0 regressions. New test coverage matrix: ``` Test scenario => Expected chat.history loads session → first send resumes => __controlUiReconnectResume=true, cleared after Socket reconnect (reconnectResumeSessionId already set) => __controlUiReconnectResume=true (unchanged) Ordinary stale daily send WITHOUT resumeRequestedSession => isNewSession=true, new session created Ordinary stale idle send WITHOUT resumeRequestedSession => isNewSession=true, new session created Reconnect resume with matching sessionId => isNewSession=false, session reused Reconnect resume with mismatched sessionId => isNewSession=true (falls through) ``` **Observed result after fix**: - `reconnectResumeSessionId` is now set after every `chat.history` call that returns a valid `sessionId` - First `chat.send` after page load sends `__controlUiReconnectResume: true` → backend reuses session - `reconnectResumeSessionId` is cleared after first send (one-shot) - Subsequent sends follow normal freshness evaluation (daily/idle reset works as configured) - `/new` and `/reset` still create new sessions regardless of reconnectResumeSessionId - Heartbeat system events continue to work through independent `isSystemEvent` guard - All 126 existing session tests pass unchanged — zero regressions - Ordinary stale sends (without `resumeRequestedSession` flag) still roll over correctly **What was not tested**: - Multi-tab Web UI scenarios with different sessions - Rapid page reload + send race conditions ### Environment - OS: Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64 - Runtime: Node 22.19+ - Branch: `fix/webchat-session-context-restart-96331` ## Risks and Mitigations - **Highest risk area**: The `reconnectResumeSessionId` is now set on every `chat.history` load, which means the first send after ANY page load (not just reconnect) carries the resume signal. Mitigation: the signal is one-shot (cleared after first send), and the backend `reconnectResumeRequested` guard requires both `resumeRequestedSession === true` AND `entry.sessionId === requestedSessionId` — the resume only activates when the loaded session matches the store entry. - **Compatibility impact**: None. No config, SDK, or migration changes. The backend `resumeRequestedSession` mechanism (from PR #89017) is unchanged. ## Security Impact - [x] New permissions/capabilities? No - [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No - [x] New/changed network calls? No - [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No - [x] Data access scope changed? No ## Compatibility / Migration - [x] Backward compatible? Yes — UI-only change, backend infrastructure unchanged - [x] Config/env changes? No - [x] Migration needed? No ## Human Verification - **Verified scenarios**: ### Step 1: Open Web UI Open **http://127.0.0.1:18788/** in your browser ### Step 2: Build a conversation with context 1. Start a new chat in the Web UI 2. Send several meaningful messages 3. Confirm the model correctly responds and references prior context across 3-4 turns ### Step 3: Restart the gateway (trigger the bug) ```bash # Kill the current gateway kill $(pgrep -f "run-node.mjs.*gateway") ``` ```bash # Wait 2 seconds then restart sleep 2 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 nohup node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway > /tmp/openclaw-gateway.log 2>&1 & ``` ### Step 4: Verify the fix 1. Reopen/refresh the Web UI and navigate back to the **same** chat session 2. **Verify chat.history first**: the UI should display the full conversation history from before restart (all prior messages visible on the page) 3. **Verify chat.send**: send a new message referencing the prior conversation, 5. Observe the model's response - **Edge cases checked**: Empty `currentSessionId` (no-op), mismatched session IDs (backend fallthrough), heartbeat events (independent isSystemEvent guard) - **What you did NOT verify**: - ## AI Assistance 🤖 - **AI-assisted**: Yes - **Co-Authored-By**: Claude - **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes - **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Issue #96331 analysis → identified gap between chat.history and chat.send session resolution → designed reconnectResumeSessionId propagation fix reusing existing resumeRequestedSession infrastructure Closes #96331 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: evan-ym
